intent: Integrate a lightweight iterative alternating optimization with gradient ascent on the forget set using uniform-target KL divergence, followed by fine-tuning on the retain set with cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation to original model predictions, improving forgetting and preserving accuracy.
rationale: This approach leverages a few gradient ascent steps on the forget set to actively push the model's predictions toward uniform, effectively erasing memorization of forget data. Alternating with fine-tuning on the retain set using cross-entropy combined with symmetric KL divergence distillation to the original model's predictions helps preserve the model's knowledge on retained and test data. Inspired by successful prior methods in the history that showed significant improvements by combining forget set ascent and retain set distillation, this method balances forgetting effectiveness with accuracy preservation while remaining computationally efficient.
